rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH ALVARDO VS. JOSEPH MCGOVERN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Mandatory Dismissal For Delay In Bringing Action To Trial
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 20, 2013, Line 1: DEFENDANTS JOSEPH MCGOVERN'S and LISA MCGOVERN'S Motion For Mandatory Dismissal For Delay In Bringing Action To Trial Granted. Cal. Code Civ. Proc. § 583.310 provides that "an action shall be brought to trial within five years after the action is commenced against the defendant." This statute is mandatory. (Wiggins v. Washington Nat. Life Ins. Co. (1966) 246 Cal.App.2d 840, 848.) The complaint was filed on November 19, 2008. Therefore, the five-year period expired on November 19, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. 44 NORMANDIE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn Costesting First, Last And Always Inc.'S Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 20, 2013, Line 2: CROSS DEFENDANT AMERICAN TERRAZZO CO.'S Motion Contesting First, Last And Always Inc.'s Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement The motion to contest is denied. American Terrazzo Co. fails to demonstrate that the settlement is "out of the ballpark" within the meaning of Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc., (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488. See CCP 877.6(d). The settlors have provided substantial evidence that the settlement is in the ballpark. It was not necessary for First, Last & Always to allocate settlement funds toward Paul Ryan's defense costs because First, Last & Always was already providing a defense to Paul Ryan. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: SEHAT SUTARDJA VS. FRANCHISE TAX BOARD, AN AGENCY OF THE STATE OF
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deft To Answer And/Or Further Answer Second Set Of Special Interrogatories And Second Set Of Document Req.'S And Req. For Sanctions Against Deft
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 20, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF SEHAT SUTARDJA'S Motion To Compel Defendant To Answer And/Or Further Answer Second Set Of Special Interrogatories And Second Set Of Document Requests And Request For Sanctions Against Defendant. Pro Tem Judge David May, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff Sutardja's Motion to Compel Defendant to Answer and/or Further Answer Second Set of Set of Document Requests and Request for Sanctions is Denied. Interrogatories in excess of 35 without required declaration. Oppressive and unduly burdensome. Seeks third party confidential information without notice to third party and in violation of law. Answers already provided are sufficient for purposes of discovery. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidedwardmay@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MAHENDRA SHAH VS. THOMAS CHASM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Entry Of Judgmnt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 20, 2013, Line 9: PLAINTIFF MAHENDRA SHAH'S Motion For Entry Of Judgment Denied. The settlement agreement attached as Exhibit A to the Declaration of Michael Heath is not signed by defendants, only defendants' attorney. CCP § 646.6 provides that a settlement agreement must be signed by the parties. "Parties" has been interpreted to mean the litigants themselves, not their attorneys or other agents. (Levy v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 10 Cal.4th 578, 585; Davidson v. Sup. Ct. (1999) 70 Cal.App.4th 514, 528.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Scott's Motion to Compel Responses to Request for Production of Documents is Denied. The retainer agreements are privileged, and that privilege has not been waived. Bus. & Prof. Code §6149. The portion of the opinion of Armandez v. Pac. Specialty Ins. Co. (2003) quoted, even if this Court could rely on it, does not hold otherwise. rulings.Case Title: ZF MICRO SOLUTIONS INC. et al VS. GORDON & REES LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Production Of Docs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 20, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANT ILLINOIS UNION INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge David May, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel ZF Micro Solutions to answer Special Interrogatories, Set One, Denied: there is no evidence that ZF Micro Solutions were served with Special Interrogatories. Motion to compel ZF Micro Solutions to respond to Request for Production of Documents, Set One; David Feldman to answer Special Interrogatories, Set One; David Feldman to respond to Request for Production of Documents, Set One, Denied: moot since plaintiff has served and lodged answers and responses without objections. Motion to compel ZF Micro Solutions and David Feldman to produce documents, Granted. The documents shall be produced at the offices of Bullivant Houser Bailey PC at 10:00 a.m. on the tenth day following filing of notice of entry of this order. If the tenth day falls on a holiday or weekend, then on the next business day. The parties are free to arrange for some other date by mutual agreement. Sanctions in the amount of $560 are awarded against plaintiffs ZF Micro Solutions and David Feldman, jointly, and in favor of Illinois Union Ins. Co. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidedwardmay@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BELLAIRE TOWER HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, VS. GOLDEN STATE BROADCASTING LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Requests For Admission, And Form Interrgoatories Or Alternatively, To Strike X-Complaint; And For Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Line 5, CROSS DEFENDANT BELLAIRE TOWER HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Requests For Admission, And Form Interrogatories Or Alternatively, To Strike Cross-Complaint; And For Sanction. Pro Tem Judge David May, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel further responses to Request for Production of Documents is Granted. As to Requests that Golden State has trouble understanding, the response should speak to documents which would fall within the category as can be understood, especially in light of Bellaire's explanations in the Separate Statement. C.C.P. §2031.220 & §2031.240. Those documents must be produced. Motion to compel further responses to Request for Admission Nos. 1, 11, 13-21 is granted. Golden State shall admit, deny or explain why it can neither admit or deny the Requests without objection. As to Request for Admission No. 22, the motion is denied. Holquin v. Sup. Ct. (Hoage) (1972) 22 Cal.App.3d 812, 820. Motion to compel further responses to Form Interrogatories Nos. 1.1, 3.3, 3.6, 3.7, 9.1(b)&(c), 9.2, 12.1, 12.3, 12.4, 12.5, 12.7, 15.1, 17.1 & 50.2 is granted. Complete answers without objections shall be provided, except if Golden State can "make a preliminary or foundational showing that answering the interrogatory would reveal the attorney's tactics, impressions, or evaluation of the case, or would result in opposing counsel taking undue advantage of the attorney's industry or efforts." Coito v. Superior Court (State) (2012) 54 Cal.4th 480, 502. The motion is denied with respect to No. 12.2. All further responses and documents shall be delivered to and received by the Law Offices of Russell Leibson no later than January 2, 2013. No sanctions are awarded. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidedwardmay@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMBILE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. MICHAEL ANTHONY TEJADA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses By Tomasico Yambao To Pltf'S Req.'S For Admission (Set 1) And Imposing Monetary Sanctions Against Tomasico Yambao And Attorney Hien D Doan
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 20, 2013, Line 6, PLAINTIFF STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMBILE INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Further Responses By Tomasico Yambao To Plaintiff's Requests For Admission (Set 1) And Imposing Monetary Sanctions Against Tomasico Yambao And Attorney Hien D Doan. Pro Tem Judge David May, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel Tomasico Yambao to provide further responses to Request for Admissions, Set One, is Denied. No sanctions awarded. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidedwardmay@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
